003 2002 2001 2000 1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 1989 1988 1987 1986 1985 1984 Skip to sidebar Hierarchical Reconfiguration of Dataflow Models Stephen Neuendorffer and Edward Lee Technical Memorandum UCB/ERL M04/2 University of Ca

echnical Memorandum UCB/ERL M04/2 University of California, Berkeley, CA 94720, USA January, 2004. ABSTRACT This paper presents a unified approach to analyzing patterns of reconfiguration in dataflow graphs. The approach is based on hierarchical dec

graphs. The approach is based on hierarchical decomposition of the structure and execution of a dataflow model. In general, reconfiguration of any part of the system might occur at any point during the execution of a model. However, arbitrary reco

the execution of a model. However, arbitrary reconfiguration must often be restricted, given the constraints of particular dataflow models of computation or modeling constructs. For instance, the reconfiguration of parameters that influence dataflo

configuration of parameters that influence dataflow scheduling or soundness of data type checking must be more heavily restricted. The paper first presents an abstract mathematical model that is sufficient to represent the reconfiguration of many ty

icient to represent the reconfiguration of many types of dataflow graphs. Using this model, a behavioral type theory is developed that bounds the points in the execution of a model when individual parameters can be reconfigured. This theory can be

rameters can be reconfigured. This theory can be used to efficiently check semantic constraints on reconfiguration, enabling the safe use of parameter reconfiguration at all levels of hierarchy. Contact Update Help Copyright © 1999 - UC Regents; all
